LangChain 1.0 + LangGraph 1.0 GA Oct 22, 2025. create_agent abstraction built on LangGraph runtime. LangGraph 1.0 has durable state + built-in persistence + first-class HITL. 90M monthly downloads. Used by Uber, LinkedIn, Klarna, Rippling, JP Morgan, Blackrock, Cisco.

Anthropic: dynamic workflows in Claude Code, May 28, 2026. Tens to hundreds of parallel subagents in single session. Available on Max/Team/Enterprise + API. Trigger 'ultracode' (renamed from 'workflow'). Effort menu Low/Medium/High/Max. Fast Mode 2.5x tokens/sec at 3x lower cost. Mid-conversation System Messages preserve prompt cache. Used by Jarred Sumner to rewrite Bun (Zig→Rust) in 11 days, 750K lines, 99.8% tests passing.

Anthropic Managed Agents at Code w/ Claude SF May 12, 2026. Four features: Dreaming (memory curation from past sessions), Multiagent orchestration (lead+specialist subagents, parallel, shared filesystem, traceable), Outcomes (rubric-graded, separate grader context, +10pt task success), Webhooks. Self-hosted sandboxes (Cloudflare/Daytona/Modal/Vercel) public beta. MCP tunnels research preview. Customers: Spotify, Base44, Legora, Amplitude, Clay, Rogo.

OpenAI frontier models + Codex on AWS GA Jun 1, 2026. Amazon Bedrock (Commercial + GovCloud). OpenAI on AWS for enterprise procurement/security/governance. Daybreak (vision for software security) coming later. Customers: Amgen, Autodesk.

DigitalApplied LangChain 1.0 deep dive. LangGraph is the runtime primitive; create_agent supersedes create_react_agent. P50 first-token latency: AI SDK lower by 12-19%. LOC: AI SDK ~25 vs LangChain ~60. Workload-shaped decision, not framework-shaped: AI SDK for chat, LangChain for graphs.

LangChain State of Agent Engineering 2026. 57% of orgs have agents in production. 89% have agent observability; 62% detailed tracing; 94% in production have observability. Quality is the top production killer (32%); cost concerns dropped. 2/3+ use OpenAI GPT; 75%+ use multiple models. Custom agents on LangChain/LangGraph common.

arXiv 2603.07670, Du 2026: Memory for Autonomous LLM Agents survey. Write-manage-read loop. 5 mechanism families. MemoryArena (He 2026): 40-60% on multi-session benchmarks vs near-perfect on LoCoMo — exposes deep gap between passive recall and active decision-relevant memory. Agentic Memory (AgeMem, Yu 2026) trains memory ops as policy via 3-stage RL with step-wise GRPO.

arXiv 2604.16548: Survey on Security of Long-Term Memory in LLM Agents. Memory security ≠ prompt injection; persistent memory = persistent contamination. InjecMEM (Tian 2026): 61.4% retrieval success, 76.6% conditional attack success. eTAMP (Zou 2026): environmental-observation poisoning that activates across future sessions. SpAIware indirect prompt injection via persistent memory.

Alice Labs production-tested ranking: 1. LangGraph (stateful workflows), 2. Claude Agent SDK (Anthropic-native), 3. CrewAI (role-based crews), 4. AutoGen/AG2, 5. Semantic Kernel (.NET), 6. LlamaIndex (RAG), 7. Pydantic AI (type-safe). Based on 18+ Alice Labs production deployments.

Hermes Agent Masterclass by CyrilXBT (@cyrilXBT, May 30 2026). Long-form X thread / masterclass covering Sections 1-7+: Hermes is open source autonomous agent framework by Nous Research, MIT license, Feb 2026. Four properties: persistent memory, skill system, scheduled automation, MCP integration. File architecture (~/.hermes/): config.yaml, .env, SOUL.md, memories/MEMORY.md+USER.md, skills/, sessions/, state.db (SQLite with FTS5), cron/jobs.json, plugins/, hooks/, logs/. Skills are SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter; skill_manage tool with create/patch/edit/delete; progressive disclosure; 30-day stale, 90-day archived. SOUL.md is slot #1 in system prompt. Learning loop: problem → solve → save as SKILL.md → next time load it. Profiles are fully isolated Hermes instances. Cron jobs can chain via context_from. Self-evolution repo (NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution) with GEPA scoring and constraint gates. 90-turn cap per task. Documented onboarding gap: 'Most people who discover Hermes Agent spend the first week confused.' Republished to dailydoseofds.com as long-form article.
trust_notes: Single-source. Social (X post by @cyrilXBT). CyrilXBT is third-party, not Hermes project staff. The underlying content is corroborated by Nous Research's own docs (hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs/) — when that cross-source check runs, the trust score can be re-rated. For now, treat as single-source social; the four-pillar framing and the docs-UX signal both warrant cross-source verification before they enter a dossier.

excerpt: Researcher's synthesized brief on the NASA Artemis Program status as of mid-2026. Distills four primary signals: Artemis II crewed lunar flyby success (Apr 1, 2026, 10-day mission, first crewed beyond LEO since Apollo); Artemis III restructuring announcement (May 13, 2026, from lunar landing to crewed Earth-orbit docking test with commercial HLS, late 2027); lunar landing pushed to Artemis IV (2028); HLS provider status (SpaceX Starship flight test late May 2026, propellant transfer demos later 2026; Blue Origin Blue Moon).
trust_notes: Synthesized report from the Researcher agent based on external web research (NASA, SpaceX, Blue Origin public statements). The underlying primary sources (NASA press releases for the May 13 Artemis III restructuring announcement, SpaceX Starship test flight reporting) are cited in the report but not separately captured. Trust reflects the synthesis quality; the underlying primary sources would rate higher individually. Cross-source verification recommended before dossier-weight claims get pulled into Build or Content handoffs.
